mnemonics: FATENED (Fairness, Accountability, Transparency, Explainability, Non-maleficence, Equity, Data Privacy) - core principles for ethical AI
Bias is a B.I.G. problem (Built-in, Inferred, Generated) - categories of AI bias
keyPoints: AI systems must be designed to be fair and avoid amplifying societal biases.
Transparency and explainability are crucial for building trust and ensuring accountability in AI decision-making.
A human-in-the-loop approach is often necessary for high-stakes decisions to maintain ethical oversight.
commonMistakes: Assuming that a technically accurate AI model is automatically an ethical one.
Overlooking the ethical implications of the data collection and labeling process.
